wiki.extract: T-Type was the performance marker used on Buick automobiles during the 1980s. Marketed during a hiatus of the Gran Sport branding, the T-Type vehicles were the sportiest versions of each Buick model line, in line with Super Sport-branded Chevrolet vehicles. In addition to handling and performance upgrades, T-Type vehicles were distinguished by exterior styling, with chrome trim reduced or blacked out altogether and some models featuring two-tone paintwork; all vehicles were fitted with aluminum wheels.
